page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i5ge.24xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The system provides 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs, utilizing a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 576.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 6.0 GB per core.

The instance includes 15,358 GB of local SSD storage, offering high-capacity, low-latency local data access. For network connectivity, the server delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 172 Gbps. There are no GPUs bundled with this instance, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the high density of local SSD storage and the dedicated CPU allocation, this instance provides a resource-dense profile for I/O-intensive workloads. The combination of 96 dedicated cores, 576.0 GB of RAM, and substantial local storage makes it highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, NoSQL databases, distributed file systems, and data warehousing applications that require high sequential read/write speeds and significant memory capacity.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i5ge.24xlarge is a high-capacity storage and database instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6982P-C processor running at 3.2 GHz, delivering 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 576.0 GB of system memory and a substantial 15,358 GB of local SSD storage. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 172 Gbps. Lacking GPU acceleration, this instance is optimized for data-dense workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, distributed file systems, and analytical data warehousing where massive local storage capacity and high network throughput are critical.

tagline: A dedicated high-core server featuring massive local SSD storage and high-speed networking for data-intensive database and storage workloads.

576.0 GB of system memory providing 6.0 GB per core.
